task_completion.description: Add the populated places layer to the project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_populated_places' should be visible in the Table of Contents (layer list) on the left side of the gvSIG interface.

task_completion.description: Open the Spatial Join or Geoprocessing tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for 'Spatial Join' or 'Geoprocessing' should be open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Set the correct target and source layers for the spatial join
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the spatial join dialog, the target layer (or layer to be joined to) must be set to 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' and the source/join layer must be set to 'ne_110m_populated_places'.

task_completion.description: Configure the spatial join to count the features
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the spatial join dialog, an option for 'Count' (or a similar summary statistic for counting intersecting features) must be selected.

task_completion.description: Set the correct output path for the resulting layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output file path text box in the spatial join or export dialog must show exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/countries_city_count.shp'.

task_completion.description: Execute the spatial join and generate the output
task_completion.visual_evidence: A progress bar completing, a success message, or the new 'countries_city_count' layer appearing in the Table of Contents, indicating the operation finished.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG Desktop GUI to perform the spatial join.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with gvSIG's graphical geoprocessing tools, rather than a terminal window running command-line GIS tools (like ogr2ogr or python scripts).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual gvSIG application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visuals of clicking through gvSIG menus, opening dialogs, and selecting layers, rather than just viewing a text file or a static image.

integrity.description: The output file was generated via the spatial join tool, not faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export path should be entered within the gvSIG geoprocessing or save dialog. The agent should not be seen creating a dummy file with the 'touch' command or simply renaming an existing file in the file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/countries_city_count.shp'. The source cities layer is located at '/home/ga/gvsig_data/cities/ne_110m_populated_places.shp'. A correct spatial join using the countries layer as the target will deterministically produce an output shapefile with exactly 177 features.
